heading: Pick a starting point
intro: These are ranges, not fixed prices — your actual quote depends on dates, room type, and availability.

categories.name: Budget-Luxury Resort
categories.description: A real private-island resort experience at the lowest resort price point — smaller rooms, simpler dining, but still your own island.
categories.bestFor: Best for: first resort trip, longer stays on a set budget

categories.name: All-Inclusive Resort
categories.description: Meals, drinks, and often excursions bundled into one price — the most predictable total cost of any resort category.
categories.bestFor: Best for: predictable budgeting, families, first-time visitors

categories.name: Family Resort
categories.description: Real kids' clubs, shallow lagoons safe for swimming, and family-configured villas — not just a regular resort with a crib added.
categories.bestFor: Best for: children under 12, multi-generation trips

categories.name: Honeymoon & Adults-Only Resort
categories.description: Water villas, private dining, and a resort culture genuinely built around couples — quiet, not family-oriented.
categories.bestFor: Best for: honeymoons, anniversaries, couples-only trips

categories.name: Premium 5-Star Resort
categories.description: Top-tier service and dining with a larger footprint — multiple restaurants, spa, watersports center, often a longer transfer.
categories.bestFor: Best for: milestone trips, longer stays, strong reef access

categories.name: Private-Island Ultra-Luxury
categories.description: Full villa privacy, butler service, and the highest-end dining — the top of the Maldives resort market.
categories.bestFor: Best for: privacy above all, special occasions, no budget ceiling
